name: node-defined
title: node-defined
summary: return the first argument that is `!== undefined`
description:
return the first argument that is `!== undefined`
Most of the time when you chain together ||s, you actually just want the
first item that is not undefined, not the first non-falsy item.
.
This module is like the defined-or (//) operator in perl 5.10+.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-defined because return the first argument that is `!== undefined`.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-defined as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-defined with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-defined --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
